Fort McMurray is experiencing extreme rapid growth.  A new Tirtiary Treatment Plant is under construction and will be operational by mid 2008.
Due to growth of the community.
Rapid growth in the community.  50% of treatment capacity was put off line to accommodate the construction of the Tertiary Treatment Plant.

comments.comment: High ammonia concentration and quantities released in year 2020 are due to the flood that affected the biology in the bioreactors decreasing the effluent water quality.  However, the bioreactors were reseeded and in year 2021 the quality of treatment has been back to normal.
Not Applicable.
Not Applicable.  No recycled quantities were reported in previous years.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: After the annual maintenance of bioreactors was completed in August 2021, phosphorous removal deteriorated for few months which resulted to higher phosphorous concentration and quantity (1.8 tonnes) of phosphorus released to the river in 2021 as compared to the previous year 2020 (1.1 tonnes).  This year (2022) phosphorous concentration in the effluent is back to normal.
Not Applicable
Not Applicable. No recycled quantities were reported in previous years.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
At the WWTP, several scheduled maintenance events occurred during the year that required diverting the influent to a storage lagoon within the facility until maintenance was completed. Naturally, due to storage and continuous biological reactions, biomass concentrations increase in wastewater.
Therefore, once maintenance is completed, new and stored wastewater fluxes are mixed in varying proportions and returned to the plant for treatment. This resulted in increased concentration.
Not Applicable
Not Applicable
